provenance.source_corpus: ADAPTIVE REVERSALS IN ACID TOLERANCE IN COPEPODS FROM LAKES RECOVERING FROM HISTORICAL STRESS

source_spans.text: Anthropogenic habitat disturbance can often lead to rapid evolution of environmental tolerances in taxa that are able to withstand the stressor.
Anthropogenic habitat disturbance can often lead to rapid evolution of environmental tolerances in taxa that are able to withstand the stressor.
On the Boreal Shield, tens of thousands of lakes acidiﬁed as a result of SO2 emissions
On the Boreal Shield, tens of thousands of lakes acidiﬁed as a result of SO2 emissions
many of these lakes are undergoing chemical recovery as a consequence of reduced emissions.
many of these lakes are undergoing chemical recovery as a consequence of reduced emissions.
L. minutus populations had higher acid tolerances in circum-neutral lakes with a history of acidiﬁcation
L. minutus populations had higher acid tolerances in circum-neutral lakes with a history of acidiﬁcation
A loss of acid tolerance in copepods from circum-neutral acid-recovering lakes was supported by a negative linear relationship
negative linear relationship between years since recovery to pH 6.0
indicated a ﬁtness trade-off in copepods with high acid tolerances at circum-neutral pH.
indicated a ﬁtness trade-off in copepods with high acid tolerances at circum-neutral pH.
L. minutus individuals had greater ﬁnal abundance in the acid-recovering lake history treatment
